title: Seed First-Party Application Baseline
context: NxGN Identity manages OIDC clients for all platform products. Actions requires four registered clients (`nxgn.actions.web`, `nxgn.actions.cli`, `nxgn.actions.mcp`, `nxgn.actions.service`) to function. Manual client creation during deployment is operationally fragile and error-prone.
Additionally, Actions must register its permission types and role templates with Identity at startup to enable authorization.
decision: **Known first-party applications are seeded at bootstrap, not created manually.**
2. Register permission types (22 permissions) with Identity
3. Register role templates (4 defaults) with Identity
4. Sync tenant list from Identity (orgs with active AMPS entitlements)
All bootstrap operations are idempotent — safe to run on every startup.
consequences: - Fresh deployments are deterministic — no manual setup steps
- Permission and role registrations stay in sync with code
- Tenant configuration automatically tracks Identity's organisation list
- Bootstrap requires Identity to be available at startup. Retry with exponential backoff; the application does not serve requests until bootstrap completes.

- Partial bootstrap failure (e.g., permissions registered but tenant sync fails) leaves the system in an inconsistent state. Mitigation: each step is idempotent and re-attempted on the next startup. Monitoring alerts on bootstrap failure.
- Four OIDC clients are required (one per interface surface: web BFF, CLI, MCP, plus M2M service-to-service). Adding or removing an interface surface requires updating the seed list.
origin: Platform convention from Identity (ADR-0001), adopted by Actions as operational necessity.
